heading:
Lapunti Arnis
De Abanico
intro: A classic Cebuano system engineered for rapid-fire execution. Lapunti pairs intricate abanico (fan) strikes with a signature cross-legged stance and footwork, maximizing torque, structural stability, and deceptive angles of attack.

characteristics.title: Abanico Strikes
characteristics.description: Rapid fan-like combinations that go around conventional blocks and defenses, striking at unexpected angles in swift succession. Honed through decades of experimentation and actual combat.

characteristics.title: Cross-Legged Footwork
characteristics.description: The only style of arnis that uses cross-legged stance and footwork. Enables swift gap-closing, angling against opponents, effective multi-opponent movement, and combat in tight spaces.

founders: Developed by the Caburnay family: Arsenio Caburnay, GM Felimon Caburnay, and SGM Prudencio 'Undo' Caburnay, with significant contributions from GM Johnny Chiuten, a master of Choy Li Fut Kung Fu and other arnis styles. Officially founded in Cebu, 1972.

acronym.label: LAPUNTI: The 3 barangays in Cebu where the Caburnay family practiced and developed the art; and the core ideals every practitioner strives to embody:
